title: Borodin-Kostochka Conjecture
statement_text: For every graph $G$ such that $\Delta(G)\geq 9$, if $\omega(G)\leq\Delta(G)-1$, then $\chi(G)\leq\Delta(G)-1$.
context_text: Borodin and Kostochka conjectured in 1977 that graphs with maximum degree at least 9 and clique number at most $\Delta(G)-1$ can be properly colored with at most $\Delta(G)-1$ colors. The assumption $\Delta(G)\geq 9$ is necessary, as shown by the graph $C_5\boxtimes K_3$.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for the stronger correspondence chromatic number $\chi_{DP}$, showing that for every graph $G$ with $\Delta(G)\geq 3\cdot 10^9$, $\chi_{DP}(G)\leq\max(\omega(G),\Delta(G)-1)$.
paper_title: On Borodin-Kostochka conjecture for correspondence coloring

title: Question 5
statement_text: What is the smallest number $\Delta_{0}$ such that every graph $G$ with $\Delta(G)\geq\Delta_{0}$ and $\omega(G)=\Delta(G)-1$ satisfies $\chi(G)=\chi_{\ell}(G)=\chi_{DP}(G)$?
context_text: Theorem 4 of this paper gives an upper bound $\Delta_0 \leq 3\cdot 10^9$ for the correspondence coloring analogue. The equality $\chi(G)=\chi_\ell(G)=\chi_{DP}(G)$ clearly holds for all graphs $G$ with $\omega(G)=\Delta(G)+1$, and the parameters also coincide for graphs with $\omega(G)=\Delta(G)$; the smallest threshold $\Delta_0$ for the case $\omega(G)=\Delta(G)-1$ remains unknown.
paper_title: On Borodin-Kostochka conjecture for correspondence coloring
